proved: The local collaboration provider registers its connected Y.Doc by project identity and removes it by exact document identity; delayed cleanup cannot remove a newer registration.

The pure inspection service rechecks live project identity, validates all heuristic projections and immutable version hashes, detects foreign-project records, validates head shape and complete bounded ancestry, and returns explicit integrity issues.
A content-valid non-head version whose ancestor is missing is detected by the collection-wide lineage graph check rather than being mistaken for healthy history.
At most 200 heuristic and 200 version metadata summaries are returned; policy text, heuristic guidance/edit values, and version notes are absent, with synthetic secret-body canaries.
The Rust MCP advertises inspect_research_state and routes it only to project.readResearchState with no caller arguments or mutation schema.

The compiled debug executable passed newline-delimited stdio discovery with 13 tools, clean stderr, current protocol negotiation, typed no-GUI status failure, installation self-description, and platform contracts.
The compiled frontend includes the live route and the structural audit locks the registry, caps, omissions, tamper checks, route, and live-harness assertion together.
The packaged mutation-capable live harness now calls the tool and requires correct project identity plus healthy integrity, but this checkpoint did not run that user-profile mutation.
notProved: The installed GUI and MCP executable completed the updated live harness against a real user profile.
